title: WSL - First invocation of  podman is breaking the JSON output with additional warnings
body:
Context 

- Windows 10/11
- WSL Ubuntu 20.04

```
~ podman --version
podman version 3.3.1
~ uname -a
Linux 5.10.60.1-microsoft-standard-WSL2 #1 SMP Wed Aug 25 23:20:18 UTC 2021 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

Upon restarting WSL and only the first time

```powershell
wsl.exe --shutdown
wsl.exe
```

The command `podman containers list --format=json` is outputting additional warning text, but this is breaking the JSON output and influences tools that rely on it. So do any other `podman` commands.

```
time="2021-10-12T13:49:18+02:00" level=warning msg="\"/\" is not a shared mount, this could cause issues or missing mounts with rootless containers"
[]
```


After invoking it the first time, all is fine

```
~ podman container list --format=json
[]
```

comments.body: Warnings and errors are written to STDERR, the command output is written to STDOUT. So if you want to parse the json you should only read STDOUT and never STDERR.
